headline: The Path to $160: The Upside Case for Walmart

headline: Sandisk Insiders Cash In on the AI Stock’s Tremendous Surge
summary: Sandisk  insiders are harvesting gains from a blistering stock rally that has been fueled by artificial-intelligence demand for the company’s memory products.  A filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission shows Chief Accounting Officer Michael Pokorny sold 2,446 shares on Tuesday for $1,426.18 each, or roughly $3.5 million.  Following the transaction, Pokorny directly owned 22,375 Sandisk shares, which were valued at just under $31 million based on Thursday’s closing price of $1,382.72.

content.title: Sandisk Insiders Cash In on the AI Stock’s Tremendous Surge
content.summary: Sandisk  insiders are harvesting gains from a blistering stock rally that has been fueled by artificial-intelligence demand for the company’s memory products.  A filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission shows Chief Accounting Officer Michael Pokorny sold 2,446 shares on Tuesday for $1,426.18 each, or roughly $3.5 million.  Following the transaction, Pokorny directly owned 22,375 Sandisk shares, which were valued at just under $31 million based on Thursday’s closing price of $1,382.72.

headline: C.H. Robinson Stock Is Actually a Better Bet After Negative SCOTUS Ruling.
summary: Shares of truck broker and third-party logistics provider  C.H. Robinson Worldwide  have had a rough few weeks, beset by all sorts of problems from artificial intelligence to  Amazon.com  to the Supreme Court.  Recent declines have made shares look more attractive to a couple of Wall Street analysts.  The problems started in February when a small company,  Algorythm Holdings, said its AI tools could organize shipping better than traditional truck brokers.

content.title: C.H. Robinson Stock Is Actually a Better Bet After Negative SCOTUS Ruling.

Recent declines have made shares look more attractive to a couple of Wall Street analysts.  The problems started in February when a small company,  Algorythm Holdings, said its AI tools could organize shipping better than traditional truck brokers.

headline: SanDisk Stock Is Up 494% This Year. Insiders Are Cashing In on the AI-Fueled Surge.
summary: Sandisk  insiders are harvesting gains from a blistering stock rally that has been fueled by artificial-intelligence demand for the company’s memory products.  A filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission shows Chief Accounting Officer Michael Pokorny sold 2,446 shares on Tuesday for $1,426.18 each, or roughly $3.5 million.  Following the transaction, Pokorny directly owned 22,375 Sandisk shares, which were valued at just under $31 million based on Thursday’s closing price of $1,382.72.

content.title: SanDisk Stock Is Up 494% This Year. Insiders Are Cashing In on the AI-Fueled Surge.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway triples Alphabet stake and invests in Delta and Macy's under new CEO
summary: Berkshire Hathaway more than tripled the size of its investment in Google's parent company and bought over $2.6 billion worth of Delta Airlines stock as Greg Abel settled into the CEO job after taking over from Warren Buffett at the start of the year.  The conglomerate also dumped a number of other stocks, including Visa, Mastercard, Domino's Pizza, Amazon and United Healthcare after the departure late last year of Todd Combs, who was one of the two investment mangers Buffett hired to help manage the portfolio.  Buffett was always reluctant to invest in tech companies because he said he didn't understand them well enough to predict the long-term winners.

content.title: Berkshire Hathaway triples Alphabet stake and invests in Delta and Macy's under new CEO
content.summary: Berkshire Hathaway more than tripled the size of its investment in Google's parent company and bought over $2.6 billion worth of Delta Airlines stock as Greg Abel settled into the CEO job after taking over from Warren Buffett at the start of the year.  The conglomerate also dumped a number of other stocks, including Visa, Mastercard, Domino's Pizza, Amazon and United Healthcare after the departure late last year of Todd Combs, who was one of the two investment mangers Buffett hired to help manage the portfolio.  Buffett was always reluctant to invest in tech companies because he said he didn't understand them well enough to predict the long-term winners.

thumbnail.caption: People walk past large portraits of Berkshire Hathaway's Warren Buffett, left, and CEO Greg Abel inside the Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting on Saturday, May 2, 2026, in Omaha, Neb. (AP Photo/Rebecca S. Gratz)

headline: Pershing Square Snapped Up Microsoft Shares, Exited Hilton, Slashed Alphabet Stake in First Quarter
summary: Pershing Square Capital Management, the hedge fund run by billionaire Bill Ackman, added  Microsoft  as a core holding during the first quarter of 2026, betting that the software giant will be a winner in the artificial-intelligence race.  Pershing Square held 11 U.S.-listed equity positions with a disclosed market value of about $13.71 billion by the end of the first quarter, according to the fund’s latest 13F filing, which was submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.  The top holding was  Brookfield  , valued at roughly $2.42 billion; followed by  Amazon  valued at $2.39 billion; and  Uber  valued at $2.15 billion.

content.title: Pershing Square Snapped Up Microsoft Shares, Exited Hilton, Slashed Alphabet Stake in First Quarter
content.summary: Pershing Square Capital Management, the hedge fund run by billionaire Bill Ackman, added  Microsoft  as a core holding during the first quarter of 2026, betting that the software giant will be a winner in the artificial-intelligence race.  Pershing Square held 11 U.S.-listed equity positions with a disclosed market value of about $13.71 billion by the end of the first quarter, according to the fund’s latest 13F filing, which was submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.  The top holding was  Brookfield  , valued at roughly $2.42 billion; followed by  Amazon  valued at $2.39 billion; and  Uber  valued at $2.15 billion.
